items.summary: Add a backend method `getDeliveryRecordsByMonth(month: Nat, year: Nat)` to the main Motoko actor that returns all DeliveryRecord entries whose date falls within the specified month and year. Also add a backend method `getMilkRecordsByMonth(month: Nat, year: Nat)` that returns all milk production records for the specified month and year.
Add React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for the two new backend methods: one to fetch delivery records by month/year and one to fetch milk production records by month/year.

Create a new Monthly Reports page at route `/reports` in `frontend/src/App.tsx`. The page must include a month/year selector (defaulting to the current month and year). It must contain two sections: (1) Monthly Delivery Report and (2) Monthly Milk Production Report.

In the Monthly Delivery Report section of the Monthly Reports page, display: (1) Summary totals card showing total deliveries count, total missed deliveries count, and total liters delivered for the selected month. (2) A per-customer breakdown table showing each customer's name, total deliveries received, total missed, and total liters. (3) A per-delivery-boy performance table showing each delivery boy's name, total deliveries made, total missed, and total liters delivered.

In the Monthly Milk Production Report section of the Monthly Reports page, display: (1) A summary card showing total liters produced for the selected month. (2) A per-cattle breakdown table showing each cattle's name/ID and total liters produced that month.

Add a print-friendly view to the Monthly Reports page. Include a 'Print Report' button that triggers `window.print()`. Apply print-specific CSS so that the navigation bar, month/year selector, and action buttons are hidden when printing, and the report content (both delivery and milk production sections) is rendered cleanly in black and white for printing.

Add a 'Reports' navigation link to the existing Layout component's top navigation bar (both desktop and mobile hamburger menu variants), pointing to the `/reports` route. Position it alongside the existing Dashboard, Cattle, Milk Production, Inventory, Customers, and Deliveries links.
